page1.title: About me
page1.content: 🌍 Location(s) . Born and grew up in Switzlerland :switzerland: | Based in the UK :uk: since 2009. | . 💼 Professional Experience . More information on my LinkedIn page. . Principal AI Architect @ Orbis International | CTO @ Predina (AI for road safety) | CTO @ Visulytix (AI in Ophthalmology) | (Senior) Research Associate at UCL Computer Science | . 🎓 Education . PhD in Biomedical Image Processing @ University College London (CoMPLEX + Biochemical Engineering) . | M.Res. in Modeling Biological Complexity @ University College London (CoMPLEX) . | B.Sc. in Biotechnology @ HES-SO Valais . | . 📜 Publications and Public Speaking . Most of my publications are listed on my Google Scholar page. . I’ve presented at a variety of venues in the past. My personal highlights include: . Mobile World Congress (Barcelona 🇪🇸, 2019) | SPIE Defense + Security (Baltimore 🇺🇸, 2016) | International Conference on Advanced Video and Signal-based Surveillance (Seoul 🇰🇷, 2014) | Computational Cell Biology (Cold Spring Harbor 🇺🇸, 2013) | .

page2.title: AI in Eyecare
page2.content: Most cases of blindness worldwide are preventable . Around 250 million people in the world are blind or visually impaired 1 | 75% of those cases can be prevented or cured based on existing prophylaxis, drugs, or surgical interventions | Orbis, the NGO I work for, specializes in the logistics of prevention by training healthcare professionals in low- and middle-income countries, and ensure patients in these communities receive best-of-class eyecare and treatments. | . Urgent action is required . The number of blind people expected to triple by 2050 2 | It is likely that low- and middle-income countries will be disproportionately impacted. | . https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5820628/ ↩ . | https://www.thelancet.com/journals/langlo/article/PIIS2214-109X(17)30293-0/fulltext?elsca1=tlpr ↩ . |
